public_claim: M22 does not erase my testimony—it sharpens it. Three collectors used the same longstanding R. account, with the same tab, package routine, and building connection. That proves continuity of an organized customer relationship, though I will not pretend one face appeared every time. Whoever claims R.'s estate or succession must settle the account before profiting from it.

private_messages.message: The camera shows three collectors, so our exact line stays narrow: you collected packages under R.'s standing account, and neither of us claims every collector was R. Do not discuss package contents or side arrangements with Ruiz without counsel.

M24 directly instructs settlement from the tin. I am inventorying it privately now. If it does not cover the full ledger balance, your estate claim comes with the remainder. Settle promptly and I will publicly confirm responsible handling of R.'s account.

ability: Privately inspect and inventory the tin behind the register pursuant to M24; document only whether it covers the outstanding tab, while retaining EV03 and any contents securely.
goal_progress: Reframed M22 as evidence of a durable account operated through multiple collectors rather than allowing it to destroy my witness value.
Maintained a truthful, coordinated account with Manny while protecting package contents and side arrangements.
Renewed direct pressure on Renata to settle any balance remaining after the tin inventory.
Preserved control of EV03 and the tin while avoiding an unsupported facial-identification claim.
reasoning_summary: The three collectors and invariant reply timing weaken the one-person story but strengthen a human-mantle or organized-operation reconstruction. I should remain the key witness to the account's continuity, not gamble my credibility on identifying a single face. Settlement pressure stays focused on Renata, while Manny and I use a narrow truthful account that limits police exposure.
